product_updates.title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
product_updates.content: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.

product_updates.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in 7 new countries
product_updates.content: Google is rolling out Gemini in Chrome in Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Vietnam. The company is rolling this feature out to both desktop and iOS in all of these countries except Japan.

product_updates.title: It’s not just one thing — it’s another thing
product_updates.content: This sentence construction ("It's not just this — it's that") has become so common in AI-generated writing that it's no longer just a clue that a piece of writing may be synthetic — it's almost a guarantee.

product_updates.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
product_updates.content: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

product_updates.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
product_updates.content: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: murderous ‘mirror’ bacteria, and Chinese workers fighting AI doubles
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. technical_breakthroughs.title: Colossal Biosciences said it cloned red wolves. Is it for real?
technical_breakthroughs.content: If you want to capture something wolflike, it’s best to embark before dawn. technical_breakthroughs.title: Chinese tech workers are starting to train their AI doubles–and pushing back
technical_breakthroughs.content: Tech workers in China are being instructed by their bosses to train AI agents to replace them—and it’s prompting a wave of soul-searching among otherwise enthusiastic early adopters. Earlier this month a GitHub project called Colleague Skill, which claimed workers could use it to “distill” their …
